rationale: Postcondition is (r1@==self_@ && r2@==self_@) ==> r1.id == r2.id, where r1.id/r2.id are Vec<u8>. The premises only give Seq-level view equality r1.id@ == r2.id@. Verus models Vec<T> via #[verifier::external_type_specification]/external_body with no axiom relating view equality to Vec spec-equality (no obeys_view_eq for raw ==; that only relates eq_spec). I confirmed this with a minimal test: assert((r1@==self_@ && r2@==self_@) ==> r1.id == r2.id) fails even when r1.id@==r2.id@ is established. clone_up_to_view is itself external_body and ensures only res@ == self@, so no further structural info about Vec id is available. The postcondition is therefore not derivable under the current Verus axiom set, matching prior attempt_01's failure. Submitting give_up.
